_meta.audioSource: Audio links use everyayah.com (full Quran) and quranicaudio.com. For specific dua audio, links direct to YouTube searches that can be replaced with actual audio files when hosting.

duas.title: The Talbiyah
duas.context: Recited continuously from the moment of entering Ihram until the first stone is thrown at Jamarat al-Aqabah.
duas.arabic: لَبَّيْكَ اللَّهُمَّ لَبَّيْكَ، لَبَّيْكَ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَكَ لَبَّيْكَ، إِنَّ الْحَمْدَ وَالنِّعْمَةَ لَكَ وَالْمُلْكَ، لاَ شَرِيكَ لَكَ
duas.transliteration: Labbayk Allahumma labbayk, labbayka la sharika laka labbayk, innal-hamda wan-ni'mata laka wal-mulk, la sharika lak
duas.translation: Here I am, O Allah, here I am. Here I am, You have no partner, here I am. Verily all praise and blessings are Yours, and all sovereignty. You have no partner.

duas.title: Intention for Umrah
duas.context: Spoken at the Miqat after donning Ihram.
duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أُرِيدُ الْعُمْرَةَ فَيَسِّرْهَا لِي وَتَقَبَّلْهَا مِنِّي
duas.transliteration: Allahumma inni uridul-'umrata fa-yassirha li wa-taqabbalha minni
duas.translation: O Allah, I intend to perform Umrah, so make it easy for me and accept it from me.

duas.title: Intention for Hajj
duas.context: Spoken at the Miqat (or in Makkah for Tamattu' pilgrims) before donning Ihram for Hajj.
duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أُرِيدُ الْحَجَّ فَيَسِّرْهُ لِي وَتَقَبَّلْهُ مِنِّي
duas.transliteration: Allahumma inni uridul-hajja fa-yassirhu li wa-taqabbalhu minni
duas.translation: O Allah, I intend to perform Hajj, so make it easy for me and accept it from me.

duas.title: Dua When Leaving Home
duas.context: Recited as you depart your home for the journey.
duas.arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، تَوَكَّلْتُ عَلَى اللَّهِ، وَلاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِاللَّهِ
duas.transliteration: Bismillahi, tawakkaltu 'alallahi, wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa billah
duas.translation: In the name of Allah, I place my trust in Allah, and there is no power nor strength except with Allah.

duas.title: Dua When Boarding (Plane, Bus, Vehicle)
duas.context: Recited when seated in any mode of transport.
duas.arabic: سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَنَا هَذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لَهُ مُقْرِنِينَ، وَإِنَّا إِلَى رَبِّنَا لَمُنْقَلِبُونَ
duas.transliteration: Subhanal-ladhi sakhkhara lana hadha wa ma kunna lahu muqrinin, wa inna ila Rabbina la-munqalibun
duas.translation: Glory to the One who has subjected this to us; we could not have done it ourselves. And indeed to our Lord we will return.

duas.title: The Traveler's Dua
duas.context: Recited at the start of the journey.
duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّا نَسْأَلُكَ فِي سَفَرِنَا هَذَا الْبِرَّ وَالتَّقْوَى، وَمِنَ الْعَمَلِ مَا تَرْضَى، اللَّهُمَّ هَوِّنْ عَلَيْنَا سَفَرَنَا هَذَا وَاطْوِ عَنَّا بُعْدَهُ، اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ الصَّاحِبُ فِي السَّفَرِ وَالْخَلِيفَةُ فِي الأَهْلِ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma inna nas'aluka fi safarina hadhal-birra wat-taqwa, wa minal-'amali ma tarda, Allahumma hawwin 'alayna safarana hadha wa atwi 'anna bu'dahu, Allahumma antas-sahibu fis-safari wal-khalifatu fil-ahl
duas.translation: O Allah, we ask You in this journey of ours for righteousness and piety, and for deeds that are pleasing to You. O Allah, make easy for us this journey and shorten its distance for us. O Allah, You are the Companion in travel and the Successor over the family.

duas.title: Dua on First Sight of the Ka'bah
duas.context: When the Ka'bah first comes into view, raise your hands and supplicate. Personal duas at this moment are answered.
duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ زِدْ هَذَا الْبَيْتَ تَشْرِيفًا وَتَعْظِيمًا وَتَكْرِيمًا وَمَهَابَةً، وَزِدْ مَنْ شَرَّفَهُ وَكَرَّمَهُ مِمَّنْ حَجَّهُ أَوِ اعْتَمَرَهُ تَشْرِيفًا وَتَكْرِيمًا وَتَعْظِيمًا وَبِرًّا
duas.transliteration: Allahumma zid hadhal-bayta tashrifan wa ta'ziman wa takriman wa mahabah, wa zid man sharrafahu wa karramahu mimman hajjahu aw i'tamarahu tashrifan wa takriman wa ta'ziman wa birra
duas.translation: O Allah, increase this House in honour, magnification, dignity and reverence. And increase those who honour and dignify it from among those who perform Hajj or Umrah here in honour, dignity, magnification and righteousness.
duas.source: Reported in classical Hajj manuals (Ibn Qudamah, others)

duas.title: Dua to Begin Each Circuit of Tawaf
duas.context: Recited at the Black Stone (or in line with it) at the start of each of the seven circuits.
duas.arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُمَّ إِيمَانًا بِكَ وَتَصْدِيقًا بِكِتَابِكَ وَوَفَاءً بِعَهْدِكَ وَاتِّبَاعًا لِسُنَّةِ نَبِيِّكَ مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّم
duas.transliteration: Bismillahi wallahu akbar. Allahumma imanan bika wa tasdiqan bi-kitabika wa wafa'an bi-'ahdika wat-tiba'an li-sunnati Nabiyyika Muhammadin sallallahu 'alayhi wa sallam
duas.translation: In the name of Allah, Allah is the Greatest. O Allah, [I begin] in faith in You, in affirmation of Your Book, in fulfilment of Your covenant, and in following the Sunnah of Your Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.
duas.source: Reported by Ibn Umar in classical sources

duas.title: Between Yemeni Corner and Black Stone
duas.context: Recited during each circuit of Tawaf between the Yemeni corner (Rukn al-Yamani) and the Black Stone.
duas.arabic: رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّار
duas.transliteration: Rabbana atina fid-dunya hasanatan wa fil-akhirati hasanatan wa qina 'adhaban-nar
duas.translation: Our Lord, grant us good in this world and good in the Hereafter, and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.

duas.title: At Maqam Ibrahim
duas.context: After completing Tawaf, pray two rakahs behind Maqam Ibrahim. Recite this verse before doing so.
duas.arabic: وَاتَّخِذُوا مِن مَّقَامِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ مُصَلًّى
duas.transliteration: Wat-takhidhu min maqami Ibrahima musalla
duas.translation: And take from the standing-place of Ibrahim a place of prayer.

duas.title: Drinking Zamzam
duas.context: Face the Qiblah, say Bismillah, drink in three breaths, and supplicate for what you wish — Zamzam is for whatever it is drunk for.
duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ عِلْمًا نَافِعًا، وَرِزْقًا وَاسِعًا، وَشِفَاءً مِنْ كُلِّ دَاءٍ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma inni as'aluka 'ilman nafi'an, wa rizqan wasi'an, wa shifa'an min kulli da'
duas.translation: O Allah, I ask You for beneficial knowledge, abundant provision, and healing from every disease.
duas.source: Sunan Ibn Majah 3062 (the dua before drinking is from the Prophet ﷺ; specific words may vary)

duas.title: Approaching Safa (Beginning Sa'i)
duas.context: Before climbing Safa for the first time at the start of Sa'i.
duas.arabic: إِنَّ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةَ مِن شَعَائِرِ اللَّه، أَبْدَأُ بِمَا بَدَأَ اللَّهُ بِهِ
duas.transliteration: Innas-Safa wal-Marwata min sha'a'irillah. Abda'u bima bada'allahu bih
duas.translation: Indeed, Safa and Marwah are among the symbols of Allah. I begin with what Allah began with.

duas.title: On Top of Safa and Marwah
duas.context: Recited three times at the top of Safa and at the top of Marwah, with personal duas in between.
duas.arabic: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ. لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ. لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ، أَنْجَزَ وَعْدَهُ، وَنَصَرَ عَبْدَهُ، وَهَزَمَ الأَحْزَابَ وَحْدَهُ
duas.transliteration: Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar. La ilaha illallah wahdahu la sharika lah, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamdu wa huwa 'ala kulli shay'in qadir. La ilaha illallahu wahdah, anjaza wa'dahu, wa nasara 'abdahu, wa hazamal-ahzaba wahdah
duas.translation: Allah is the Greatest, Allah is the Greatest, Allah is the Greatest. There is no god but Allah alone, with no partner; to Him belongs sovereignty and to Him belongs all praise, and He has power over all things. There is no god but Allah alone; He fulfilled His promise, gave victory to His servant, and defeated the confederates alone.

duas.title: The Best Dua of the Day of Arafah
duas.context: The Prophet ﷺ said the best dua is the dua of the Day of Arafah, and the best of what he and the Prophets before him said is this.
duas.arabic: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
duas.transliteration: La ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lah, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamd, wa huwa 'ala kulli shay'in qadir
duas.translation: There is no god but Allah alone, with no partner. To Him belongs sovereignty and to Him belongs all praise, and He has power over all things.

duas.title: Comprehensive Dua at Arafah
duas.context: A widely-used supplication during the long afternoon at Arafah.
duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لَكَ الْحَمْدُ كَالَّذِي تَقُولُ وَخَيْرًا مِمَّا نَقُولُ، اللَّهُمَّ لَكَ صَلاَتِي وَنُسُكِي وَمَحْيَايَ وَمَمَاتِي، وَإِلَيْكَ مَآبِي، وَلَكَ رَبِّ تُرَاثِي. اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ عَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ، وَوَسْوَسَةِ الصَّدْرِ، وَشَتَاتِ الأَمْرِ. اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ خَيْرِ مَا تَجِيءُ بِهِ الرِّيحُ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا تَجِيءُ بِهِ الرِّيحُ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma lakal-hamdu kalladhi taqulu wa khayran mimma naqul. Allahumma laka salati wa nusuki wa mahyaya wa mamati, wa ilayka ma'abi, wa laka Rabbi turathi. Allahumma inni a'udhu bika min 'adhabil-qabri, wa waswasatis-sadri, wa shatatil-amr. Allahumma inni as'aluka min khayri ma taji'u bihir-rih, wa a'udhu bika min sharri ma taji'u bihir-rih
duas.translation: O Allah, all praise is Yours as You have said and better than what we say. O Allah, my prayer, my sacrifice, my life and my death are for You; to You is my return, and to You belongs my legacy. O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the punishment of the grave, the whisperings of the chest, and the dispersion of affairs. O Allah, I ask You for the good of what the wind brings, and seek refuge in You from the evil of what the wind brings.
duas.source: Reported by Ali ibn Abi Talib; Jami' at-Tirmidhi

duas.title: At Mash'ar al-Haram (Muzdalifah)
duas.context: Recited at Muzdalifah after Fajr, facing the Qiblah, raising hands.
duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ كَمَا وَقَّفْتَنَا فِيهِ وَأَرَيْتَنَا إِيَّاهُ، فَوَفِّقْنَا لِذِكْرِكَ كَمَا هَدَيْتَنَا، وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا وَارْحَمْنَا
duas.transliteration: Allahumma kama wawaqqaftana fihi wa araytana iyyah, fa-waffiqna li-dhikrika kama hadaytana, waghfir lana warhamna
duas.translation: O Allah, just as You have made us stop here and shown it to us, grant us success in remembering You as You have guided us, and forgive us and have mercy upon us.

duas.title: At Each Pebble Thrown (Rami)
duas.context: Said with each of the seven pebbles thrown at each Jamarah.
duas.arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، رَغْمًا لِلشَّيْطَانِ وَرِضًا لِلرَّحْمَن
duas.transliteration: Bismillahi, wallahu akbar, raghman lish-shaytani wa ridan lir-Rahman
duas.translation: In the name of Allah, Allah is the Greatest. In defiance of Satan and to please the Most Merciful.
duas.source: Reported in classical Hajj manuals; the takbir is mutawatir

duas.title: After Jamarah al-Sughra and al-Wusta
duas.context: After completing the small and middle Jamarat (on the 11th, 12th, 13th), step aside, face Qiblah, raise hands and supplicate. No supplication after Jamarah al-Aqabah (the largest).
duas.arabic: Make personal duas. Many scholars recommend reciting Surah al-Fatihah and Ayat al-Kursi.
duas.translation: Pause and make extensive personal supplication.

duas.title: At the Multazam
duas.context: The Multazam is the area between the Black Stone and the door of the Ka'bah. Press the chest, face, and arms against the wall and supplicate.
duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ بِأَنَّكَ أَنْتَ اللَّهُ الَّذِي لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ أَنْت
duas.transliteration: Allahumma inni as'aluka bi'annaka antallah, alladhi la ilaha illa ant
duas.translation: O Allah, I ask You by virtue that You are Allah, there is no god but You.
duas.source: Recommended in classical sources for personal supplication

duas.title: Returning Home
duas.context: Said when returning from the journey, while looking at one's home or town.
duas.arabic: آيِبُونَ، تَائِبُونَ، عَابِدُونَ، لِرَبِّنَا حَامِدُون
duas.transliteration: Ayibun, ta'ibun, 'abidun, li-Rabbina hamidun
duas.translation: We return, we repent, we worship, and we praise our Lord.

duas.title: Sayyid al-Istighfar (The Master of Seeking Forgiveness)
duas.context: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever says it during the day with conviction and dies that day before evening will be of the people of Paradise; and whoever says it during the night with conviction and dies before morning will be of the people of Paradise.'
duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ رَبِّي لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ، خَلَقْتَنِي وَأَنَا عَبْدُكَ، وَأَنَا عَلَى عَهْدِكَ وَوَعْدِكَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُ، أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا صَنَعْتُ، أَبُوءُ لَكَ بِنِعْمَتِكَ عَلَيَّ، وَأَبُوءُ بِذَنْبِي، فَاغْفِرْ لِي فَإِنَّهُ لاَ يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلاَّ أَنْت
duas.transliteration: Allahumma anta Rabbi, la ilaha illa ant. Khalaqtani wa ana 'abduk, wa ana 'ala 'ahdika wa wa'dika mas-tata't. A'udhu bika min sharri ma sana't. Abu'u laka bi-ni'matika 'alayy, wa abu'u bi-dhanbi, faghfir li, fa-innahu la yaghfirudh-dhunuba illa ant
duas.translation: O Allah, You are my Lord, there is no god but You. You created me and I am Your servant. I keep Your covenant and Your promise as much as I am able. I seek refuge in You from the evil I have done. I acknowledge Your blessings upon me and I acknowledge my sin. Forgive me, for none forgives sins except You.
